40.reason: Error: Format specifier "%1c" is incorrect; it should be "%c". "%1c" may cause undefined behavior or incorrect output.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Format specifier "%1c" is incorrect; it should be "%c". "%1c" may cause undefined behavior or incorrect output.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The format specifier "%1c" is incorrect; it should be "%c". "%1c" may cause undefined behavior or incorrect output.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  // %1c is unnecessary; just %c suffices. More critically, if t isn't properly null-terminated or le is wrong, output may be corrupted.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  // "%1c" is unnecessary; just "%c" suffices. Though not a compilation error, combined with uninitialized/undefined t content, output may be wrong.

15.reason: Logic error: the condition does not correctly check that both characters are in the same character range (lowercase, uppercase, or digit) and also lacks the necessary check that s[i+1] > s[i-1]. It may incorrectly process mixed types or equal characters.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The condition is incorrect: it should check that both characters are within the same range, not just that the first is >= start and the second is <= end. For example, 'a' <= s[i-1] <= 'z' AND 'a' <= s[i+1] <= 'z' should be checked separately. Also missing parentheses around each range check.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Condition does not ensure both characters are of the same type and does not check s[i+1] > s[i-1]; also operator precedence may cause incorrect evaluation.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Logic error: the condition does not correctly check that both characters are in the same character range (lowercase, uppercase, or digit) and also lacks the necessary check that s[i+1] > s[i-1]. It may incorrectly process mixed types or equal characters.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Logic error: Fails to verify that both characters are of the same type (e.g., 'a' and 'Z' incorrectly pass) and does not check if the right character is strictly greater than the left character.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Missing check for s[i+1] > s[i-1] and incomplete range validation; should ensure both characters are strictly within the same type range and the right character is greater than the left.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  // Condition logic is incorrect: it checks only lower bound of left char and upper bound of right char, not that both are in the same category. Also missing parentheses around each category condition leading to wrong operator precedence.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  // Condition is incorrect: it checks only lower bound for left char and upper bound for right char, but doesn't ensure both chars are of the same category (e.g., 'a' and 'Z' would incorrectly pass). Also missing parentheses around each category condition leading to wrong operator precedence.

25.reason: This decrement of j is incorrect; it causes the next character to overwrite the last expanded character. The j should not be decremented here.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This decrement of j is incorrect; it causes the next character to overwrite the last expanded character. The j should not be decremented here.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This decrement is incorrect when no expansion should occur (e.g., c<=0 or types mismatch), causing the dash to be skipped.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  // This decrements j unnecessarily; after filling expansion chars, j should point to next empty slot, but this breaks alignment for next character.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  // This decrements j after incrementing it in the loop, which may cause overwriting or skipping characters in some cases; logic for index management is flawed.

35.reason: Missing null terminator for t array; subsequent strlen(t) will read uninitialized memory.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Missing null terminator for t array; subsequent strlen(t) will read uninitialized memory.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Missing null terminator assignment (t[j+1] = '\0';) after copying the last character, causing strlen(t) to potentially read out of bounds.

13.reason: When the expansion condition is not met, the '-' character is not copied to t, causing it to be omitted from the output.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  When the expansion condition is not met, the '-' character is not copied to t, causing it to be omitted from the output.

36.reason: The array t is not explicitly null-terminated before calling strlen, which may cause undefined behavior or incorrect string length calculation.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The array t is not explicitly null-terminated before calling strlen, which may cause undefined behavior or incorrect string length calculation.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Using strlen on a string that may not be null-terminated leads to undefined behavior or incorrect length calculation.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  // strlen(t) is unsafe because t may not be null-terminated, leading to undefined behavior.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  // strlen(t) is unreliable because t is not null-terminated before this point, leading to undefined behavior.
